content:
## Selecting the Card to Recommend
Recommend the credit-card product using eligible_card_with_highest_annual_net_value. Only compare cards for which the customer is eligible under the product terms; do not use this page to relax a product threshold or add a card that is not in the catalog. See "Credit Card Product Catalog Overview" for the available cards and "Credit Card Fee Apr Rewards Matrix" for values needed to estimate annual net value.

## Tie Handling
When two or more eligible cards produce the same annual net value, apply the tie breakers in this exact order: lower_annual_fee, lower_purchase_apr, lexicographic_card_name. Stop as soon as one rule selects a single card. If the first rule does not resolve the tie, continue to the next rule rather than presenting multiple recommendations.

## Credit Tier Ranking
Use the rank value only to order tiers for eligibility comparisons:

| credit tier | rank |
|---|---:|
| no_score | 0 |
| poor | 1 |
| fair | 2 |
| good | 3 |
| excellent | 4 |

A higher rank is treated as a stronger tier for comparison. If a customer’s credit tier is missing, ask for that single missing value before recommending a card.

## Customer Answering Notes
Explain the recommendation in customer-facing terms: eligibility first, then annual net value, then any tie breaker that affected the answer. For employee-only steps or application handling, refer to "Internal: Credit Card Recommendation Policy" and "Internal: Credit Card Application User Tool Doc" rather than describing internal workflow details to the customer.
title: Card Recommendation FAQ
